scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The Coverage-areas panel renders a real map (good), but offers no affordance to confirm WHICH polygon is which ward — the two ward fills are near-identical pastels with no per-polygon labels or legend — so the core 'confirm the exact coverage the firm will bid on' interaction is incomplete. The rest of the solicitation form is also an empty shell, so a worked example of a real call is only half-shown.
fix_recommendation: Add per-polygon map labels ('Attakar', 'Gura') and a small color legend in the panel header so the owner can verify ward identity on the map itself; pre-fill the title/description/dates with the R6 example so the scene shows a real solicitation being created, not a blank shell.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The green (Attakar) and blue (Gura) ward fills are too close in hue/value to read as two distinct, named areas — the one place color is load-bearing — and several empty stub sections (Application Questions, Evaluation Criteria, all dates) leave large dead whitespace blocks.
fix_recommendation: Increase the fill-color contrast between the two wards (saturated green vs saturated blue) and add legend swatches tying color to ward name.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: ADVISORY (non-blocking). Narration claims a Coverage panel that DRAWS the two wards as a map with 'Attakar green / Gura blue'. The map IS present with two polygons (claim largely demonstrated), but the specific per-ward color identity is not verifiable on-screen — the two fills are near-identical and unlabeled.
fix_recommendation: Resolved by the scene-2 legend + contrast fix (saturated green/blue fills, on-map labels, legend swatches). Note for later triage; does not gate the verdict.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Core launch affordance is coherent (primary 'Create solicitation' + secondary 'Open study'), but two top-bar actions ('Compare selected', '+ Group selected plans') appear disabled with no narration of the precondition, and the consequence of the primary action isn't previewed — a mild dead-end-adjacent ambiguity.
fix_recommendation: Add helper text / tooltip on the disabled buttons stating the precondition (e.g. 'Select plans to compare or group'); optionally preview what 'Create solicitation' carries forward (e.g. 'Source: R6 — Attakar × Gura').

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Functional and clean but unremarkable: one study card floats over ~70% empty whitespace below the fold, and the card stacks four redundant 'done' signals (STUDY pill, READY TO SOLICIT pill, 'reviewed' text, and a green 'Finished study — ready to recruit' line) — three ways of saying the same state.
fix_recommendation: Collapse the three 'done' signals into a single badge, and surface the collapsed 'Candidate plans in progress (9)' inline (a compact list/grid) to fill the viewport instead of one card over a half-empty page.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Functional and clean, but the token 'Awarded' prints three times on a single row (STATUS, RECOMMENDATION, and the ACTIONS column's 'Awarded' indicator), reading repetitive; and the captured cursor collides with a clipped/truncated breadcrumb crumb ('...Household survey firm — Round 6 (Attakar × ...'). Note: the awarded row's ACTIONS are now coherent (View + Awarded only, NO contradictory live Review action) — the prior-iteration interaction defect is resolved.
fix_recommendation: Differentiate the ACTIONS 'Awarded' lockup to a checkmark-only indicator (distinct from the STATUS badge) so the same word doesn't render three times per row; add proper ellipsis padding to the breadcrumb clear of the cursor zone.
